alkylation reagent: alkylated with 100 mM iodoacetamide in the dark at room temperature.
alkylation concentration: alkylated with 100 mM iodoacetamide in the dark at room temperature.
cleavage agent: samples were sequentially digested by Lys-C protease at 37 °C for 3 h, followed by trypsin at 37 °C overnight.
collision energy: The normalized collision energy was set to 30% for HCD scans.
fragmentation method: Fragmentation (MS2) spectra were acquired in the Orbitrap at 60 K resolution.
instrument: connected to a Thermo Orbitrap Fusion Lumos Tribrid Mass Spectrometer.
labeling: phosphatase activity was measured using the phospho-EGFR peptides.
reduction reagent: assay buffer (25 mM Hepes pH 7.4, 50 mM NaCl, and 1 mM DTT).
reduction concentration: assay buffer (25 mM Hepes pH 7.4, 50 mM NaCl, and 1 mM DTT).
